meta.title: Quiz 4 — REST, Databases & Security
meta.description: REST API design, database concepts, authentication, cookies, sessions, and security.

questions.questionMd: By default, without any additional options, which HTTP method does the `fetch()` API use when making a request?

options.label: A
options.text: POST

options.label: B
options.text: GET

options.label: C
options.text: DELETE

options.label: D
options.text: PUT

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: A developer receives the string `'{"name":"Ice Bear"}'` from a server. Which function should they use to turn it into a usable JavaScript object?

options.label: A
options.text: JSON.objectify()

options.label: B
options.text: JSON.decode()

options.label: C
options.text: JSON.parse()

options.label: D
options.text: JSON.stringify()

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: In REST API terminology, what is the "base URL"?

options.label: A
options.text: The full URL including all query parameters for a specific request

options.label: B
options.text: The fallback URL used when the primary endpoint is unavailable

options.label: C
options.text: The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended

options.label: D
options.text: The URL of the web page that consumes the API

questions.explanationMd: The base URL is the common prefix for the routing system of a REST application.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, which company originally developed and used GraphQL before open-sourcing it?

options.label: A
options.text: GitHub

options.label: B
options.text: Netflix

options.label: C
options.text: Facebook

options.label: D
options.text: Spotify

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: Which built-in JavaScript array method is used in the lecture to reorder album objects by year?

options.label: A
options.text: .reduce()

options.label: B
options.text: .map()

options.label: C
options.text: .filter()

options.label: D
options.text: .sort()

questions.explanationMd: The `.sort()` method directly handles repositioning array items via a comparator function.

questions.questionMd: Why is `encodeURIComponent()` used on a user's search query before appending it to a fetch URL?

options.label: A
options.text: To ensure special characters in the query string are properly percent-encoded and do not break the URL

options.label: B
options.text: To encrypt the user's input before sending it to the server

options.label: C
options.text: To trim whitespace from the beginning and end of the search text

options.label: D
options.text: To convert the query string into a JSON-compatible format

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does MIME type (content type) specify in the context of a RESTful API request?

options.label: A
options.text: The maximum size of the response body allowed

options.label: B
options.text: The HTTP method that must be used for the request

options.label: C
options.text: The authentication scheme required to access the endpoint

options.label: D
options.text: The format of the data being sent or returned, such as JSON, HTML, or plain text

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: What is the fundamental difference between a URL that points to a static file and a URL that represents a REST API endpoint?

options.label: A
options.text: A static file URL always downloads a file, API endpoints render in the browser.

options.label: B
options.text: REST APIs require a VPN.

options.label: C
options.text: A static file requires authentication.

options.label: D
options.text: A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response...

questions.explanationMd: REST APIs are applications running on a server that process requests and generate responses on-the-fly, as opposed to a web server simply serving a flat file off a disk.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the `fetch()` function in JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: To serialize JavaScript objects into JSON strings

options.label: B
options.text: To parse HTML documents and manipulate the DOM

options.label: C
options.text: To load resources asynchronously over a network using a Promise-based approach

options.label: D
options.text: To synchronously read files from the local file system

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: The lecture recommends implementing the `Album` class before the `App` class. What is the stated reason for this order?

options.label: A
options.text: The fetch() call can only be tested after the Album class exists

options.label: B
options.text: The App class depends on the Album class, whereas the Album class has no dependencies

options.label: C
options.text: JavaScript requires dependent classes to be declared first in the file

options.label: D
options.text: The Album class is simpler and requires fewer lines of code

questions.explanationMd: Good architectural practice typically implements self-contained, dependency-free models (`Album`) before wiring up higher-level coordination logic (`App`).

questions.questionMd: How does the lecture characterize Netflix's Falcor compared to GraphQL as an alternative to REST?

options.label: A
options.text: Falcor is described as the most widely adopted REST alternative in the industry

options.label: B
options.text: Falcor is described as a synchronous alternative to REST that avoids HTTP entirely

options.label: C
options.text: Falcor is described as likely interesting but rarely used in practice and lacking a Wikipedia page

options.label: D
options.text: Falcor is described as the predecessor to GraphQL that Facebook later adopted

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture's completed fetch example, which JavaScript expression creates a new image element equivalent to `document.createElement('img')`?

options.label: A
options.text: new Image()

options.label: B
options.text: document.newImage()

options.label: C
options.text: new Img()

options.label: D
options.text: Image.create()

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: To allow a form to submit when a user presses the Enter key, which event should be listened for on the `<form>` element?

options.label: A
options.text: click

options.label: B
options.text: submit

options.label: C
options.text: change

options.label: D
options.text: keydown

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: In the discography page example, how does the `SortButton` class communicate a sort action back to the `App` class?

options.label: A
options.text: It dispatches a custom keyboard event that triggers the App's event listener

options.label: B
options.text: It calls an onClickCallback function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument

options.label: C
options.text: It directly modifies the albumInfo property of the App instance

options.label: D
options.text: It fires a built-in DOM click event that the App class listens for on the document

questions.explanationMd: Passing callbacks as constructors props enables decoupled classes to communicate events back up to their parents.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?

options.label: A
options.text: function(a, b) { return b.year - a.year; }

options.label: B
options.text: function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }

options.label: C
options.text: function(a, b) { return a.year < b.year; }

options.label: D
options.text: function(a, b) { return a.year; }

questions.explanationMd: Array `.sort()` determines order based on whether the return value is negative, zero, or positive. `a.year - b.year` sorts numerically in ascending order.

questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain such as `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function `b` receive as its argument?

options.label: A
options.text: The original URL string passed to fetch()

options.label: B
options.text: The value returned by function a

options.label: C
options.text: The HTTP status code of the response

options.label: D
options.text: The raw HTTP response object

questions.explanationMd: The return value of a preceding `.then()` block is passed as the argument to the subsequent `.then()` block.

questions.questionMd: A developer receives the string `'{"name": "Ice Bear"}'` from a server. Which function should they use to turn it into a usable JavaScript object?

options.label: A
options.text: JSON.objectify()

options.label: B
options.text: JSON.parse()

options.label: C
options.text: JSON.decode()

options.label: D
options.text: JSON.stringify()

questions.explanationMd: `JSON.parse()` reads a JSON string and translates it directly to standard JS properties and objects.

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.label: A
options.text: JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files

options.label: B
options.text: JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing

options.label: C
options.text: JSON files do not require a server to be accessed

options.label: D
options.text: JSON is the only format supported by the fetch() API

questions.explanationMd: Data mapping and typecasting (like Strings vs Numbers) handle themselves implicitly through `JSON.parse()`.

questions.questionMd: When loading album data from a custom plain-text file, which of the following is a disadvantage mentioned in the lecture?

options.label: A
options.text: Text files do not support arrays of items

options.label: B
options.text: Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like parseInt()

options.label: C
options.text: Plain-text files cannot be served over HTTP

options.label: D
options.text: The file cannot be loaded using the fetch() API

questions.explanationMd: Since all content in a plain text file is read as purely textual string data, manual casting functions are needed to parse numbers accurately.

questions.questionMd: Why is the `fetch()` API described as asynchronous in the context of web programming?

options.label: A
options.text: Because it runs only on multi-core processors

options.label: B
options.text: Because it blocks the UI thread until the network responds

options.label: C
options.text: It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response.

options.label: D
options.text: Because it forces the server to respond immediately

questions.explanationMd: Asynchronous operations operate independently of the main program flow, ensuring the user interface remains responsive while network requests process in the background.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a JavaScript Promise?

options.label: A
options.text: An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation

options.label: B
options.text: A synchronous function that immediately returns a value

options.label: C
options.text: A built-in timer that delays code execution by a specified amount

options.label: D
options.text: A special array type used to store multiple fetch results

questions.explanationMd: This is the classic Mozilla Developer Network definition of a Promise.

questions.questionMd: Why does the lecture suggest extracting the album rendering logic into a separate `_renderAlbums()` helper method?

options.label: A
options.text: To avoid exceeding the maximum allowed number of lines in a single method

options.label: B
options.text: So the same rendering code can be reused both after the initial fetch and after each sort operation

options.label: C
options.text: To prevent the browser from caching the album images between renders

options.label: D
options.text: Because the fetch() API requires a dedicated render callback

questions.explanationMd: Extracting rendering logic allows you to re-call it anytime state changes without rewriting code (DRY - Don't Repeat Yourself).

questions.questionMd: When constructing a URL with multiple query parameters, which character is used to separate each parameter after the first one?
questions.explanationMd: The `?` starts the query string and `&` separates additional parameter key-value pairings.

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method does a browser send automatically whenever you navigate to a URL in the address bar?

options.label: A
options.text: PUT

options.label: B
options.text: GET

options.label: C
options.text: DELETE

options.label: D
options.text: POST

questions.explanationMd: Browser address bar navigation always performs an HTTP GET request to fetch page contents.

questions.questionMd: Which command is used to serve local files over HTTP so that `fetch()` can access them during development?

options.label: A
options.text: npm start http

options.label: B
options.text: node server.js

options.label: C
options.text: python3 -m fetch server

options.label: D
options.text: python3 -m http.server

questions.explanationMd: Python 3 has a built-in lightweight HTTP server module useful for quick local development testing without CORS/File protocol issues.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.label: A
options.text: To develop web browsers and operating systems

options.label: B
options.text: To act as a central governing body for the Internet

options.label: C
options.text: To provide internet connection services globally

options.label: D
options.text: To develop protocols and guidelines that ensure long-term growth for the Web

questions.explanationMd: The W3C is an international community where member organizations, a full-time staff, and the public work together to develop Web standards.

questions.questionMd: In the context of the WWW, what is hypertext?

options.label: A
options.text: A protocol for securely transferring files between computers

options.label: B
options.text: A database format used to store website settings

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language used to style web page layouts

options.label: D
options.text: Text that contains links to other texts or documents

questions.explanationMd: Hypertext is text displayed on a computer display or other electronic devices with references (hyperlinks) to other text that the reader can immediately access.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.label: A
options.text: SGML is a style sheet language used to format HTML documents.

options.label: B
options.text: HTML was completely replaced by SGML in modern web development.

options.label: C
options.text: SGML is a standard for creating markup languages, and early versions of HTML were based on it.

options.label: D
options.text: SGML is a scripting language that runs in the browser.

questions.explanationMd: SGML is the standard from which early versions of HTML were derived. It defines the rules for creating markup languages.

questions.questionMd: Which tag must be used as the root element of an HTML document?

options.label: A
options.text: <head>

options.label: B
options.text: <body>

options.label: C
options.text: <!DOCTYPE html>

options.label: D
options.text: <html>

questions.explanationMd: The `<html>` element is the root element of an HTML page, containing all other elements.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet, respectively?

options.label: A
options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranets), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet

options.label: B
options.text: LANs are strictly used for the Internet, while WANs are strictly used for Intranets

options.label: C
options.text: LANs operate over wireless networks, while WANs operate over wired networks

options.label: D
options.text: Both LANs and WANs are entirely isolated from the global internet

questions.explanationMd: Local Area Networks (LANs) typically make up private corporate Intranets, while Wide Area Networks (WANs) connect these smaller networks globally to form the Internet.

questions.questionMd: Which statement is TRUE regarding the history of HTTP/2?

options.label: A
options.text: HTTP/2 requires encrypted connections by default.

options.label: B
options.text: HTTP/2 removed all headers used in HTTP/1.1.

options.label: C
options.text: HTTP/2 was designed to overcome performance limitations of HTTP/1.1 without changing its semantics.

options.label: D
options.text: HTTP/2 only supports text-based communication, not binary.

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 introduced features like multiplexing to speed up page loads without altering the core semantics (methods, status codes, URIs) of HTTP.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was initially created by which organization, under what name, and for what primary purpose?

options.label: A
options.text: European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)

options.label: B
options.text: World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)

options.label: C
options.text: Department of Defense's Advanced Research Projects Agency (ARPA); ARPANET; military communication and research

options.label: D
options.text: The Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF)

questions.explanationMd: The Internet began as ARPANET, funded by the US Department of Defense's ARPA, to build a robust, fault-tolerant communication network.

questions.questionMd: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN?

options.label: A
options.text: Steve Jobs

options.label: B
options.text: Tim Berners-Lee

options.label: C
options.text: Bill Gates

options.label: D
options.text: Alan Turing

questions.explanationMd: Sir Tim Berners-Lee is credited with inventing the World Wide Web while working at CERN in 1989.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a primary goal of CSS?

options.label: A
options.text: To add interactive logic to web pages

options.label: B
options.text: To query databases for web data

options.label: C
options.text: To separate the presentation of a document from its structure and content

options.label: D
options.text: To define the HTML tags on a page

questions.explanationMd: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) was designed to separate document content (HTML) from document presentation (layout, colors, fonts).

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method is considered "idempotent" by default?

options.label: A
options.text: POST

options.label: B
options.text: PATCH

options.label: C
options.text: GET

options.label: D
options.text: CONNECT

questions.explanationMd: An idempotent HTTP method is one that can be called many times without producing different outcomes. GET is idempotent; it simply retrieves data.

questions.questionMd: In web development, what is a "port"?

options.label: A
options.text: A physical connection defining hardware interfaces

options.label: B
options.text: A function that holds an IP address

options.label: C
options.text: A communication endpoint mapped to a specific process or network service

options.label: D
options.text: A standard URL parameter

questions.explanationMd: A port is a virtual point where network connections start and end. It is managed by the OS and helps direct traffic to the correct application.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://www.example.com:8080`, what does the `8080` represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The TCP port number the server is listening on

options.label: B
options.text: The subnet mask of the server IP address

options.label: C
options.text: The file size allocated for the document request

options.label: D
options.text: The maximum payload size of the HTTP request

questions.explanationMd: The number following the colon after the hostname in a URL specifies the port number on which the server is listening for traffic.

questions.questionMd: How is a static method defined on a class in modern JS?

options.label: A
options.text: By using the `static` keyword before the method name inside the class body

options.label: B
options.text: By using the `@static` decorator before the class definition

options.label: C
options.text: By placing the method outside of the class block

options.label: D
options.text: By listing them as parameters in the class constructor line

questions.explanationMd: In JavaScript, you define static methods by prepending the `static` keyword to the method name within a class definition.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary benefit of the `<canvas>` element in HTML5?

options.label: A
options.text: It acts as a layout container for multiple HTML components at the same time

options.label: B
options.text: It provides a responsive iframe to embed other websites easily

options.label: C
options.text: It handles complex form submissions securely without a server

options.label: D
options.text: It provides a drawing surface that is manipulated via JavaScript for rendering 2D and 3D graphics

questions.explanationMd: The `<canvas>` element acts as a container for graphics, requiring a script (usually JavaScript) to draw the graphics programmatically.

questions.questionMd: What happens to the variables declared inside a function when the function finishes executing (assuming no closures)?

options.label: A
options.text: They are immediately garbage collected after the outer function has finished

options.label: B
options.text: They are kept alive forever just in case the function is called again

options.label: C
options.text: They are saved to LocalStorage by the browser

options.label: D
options.text: They become global variables accessible anywhere in the program

questions.explanationMd: Local variables within a function scope are generally cleared from memory (garbage collected) once the function's execution context ends, unless a closure keeps a reference to them.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://www.example.com/api/users?id=123`, what does the `?id=123` portion represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The file path

options.label: B
options.text: The protocol

options.label: C
options.text: The domain extension

options.label: D
options.text: The query string/parameters

questions.explanationMd: The portion of a URL following the question mark (`?`) is the query string, which assigns values to specified parameters.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is the fundamental difference between a single-page application (SPA) and a multi-page application (MPA)?

options.label: A
options.text: An SPA is much slower than an MPA due to heavy JavaScript use.

options.label: B
options.text: An SPA updates portions of the page dynamically using JavaScript, while an MPA requires a full page reload for every interaction.

options.label: C
options.text: An SPA can only have one HTML file, and no server backend.

options.label: D
options.text: MPAs are highly secure, while SPAs cannot utilize HTTPS.

questions.explanationMd: SPAs load a single HTML page and dynamically update content as the user interacts, whereas MPAs navigate to completely new HTML pages from the server.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following HTTP headers is used to indicate the media type of the resource?

options.label: A
options.text: User-Agent

options.label: B
options.text: Accept

options.label: C
options.text: Host

options.label: D
options.text: Content-Type

questions.explanationMd: The `Content-Type` header tells the client what the content type of the returned content actually is (e.g., text/html, application/json).

questions.questionMd: How is an HTML input element's current value typically retrieved in JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: By calling `input.getValue()` method

options.label: B
options.text: By parsing the `innerHTML` of the element

options.label: C
options.text: By accessing the `value` property of the input element's DOM object.

options.label: D
options.text: By reading the `data-val` attribute

questions.explanationMd: Form inputs expose a `.value` property on their DOM node representation that contains the user's input.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.label: A
options.text: JSON files compress perfectly without algorithms

options.label: B
options.text: JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object...

options.label: C
options.text: JSON does not require a web server to be fetched

options.label: D
options.text: JSON inherently blocks Cross-Site Scripting attacks

questions.explanationMd: Since JSON syntax is derived from JavaScript object notation, native functions like `JSON.parse()` make it extremely easy to deserialize.

questions.questionMd: Given the object `let album = { title: "Daydream", year: 1995 };`, how would you access the year of the album?

options.label: A
options.text: album[1]

options.label: B
options.text: album.get('year')

options.label: C
options.text: album.year

options.label: D
options.text: album->year

questions.explanationMd: In JavaScript, object properties are accessed using dot notation (e.g., `object.property`) or bracket notation.

questions.questionMd: What is the event fired when an input field's value changes and it loses focus?

options.label: A
options.text: click

options.label: B
options.text: submit

options.label: C
options.text: keyPress

options.label: D
options.text: change

questions.explanationMd: The `change` event is fired for `<input>`, `<select>`, and `<textarea>` elements when the user modifies the element's value and then blurs (loses focus) from it.

questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain like `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function `b` receive as its argument?

options.label: A
options.text: The original URL string

options.label: B
options.text: The HTTP response status code

options.label: C
options.text: The value returned by function `a`

options.label: D
options.text: The Promise object itself

questions.explanationMd: In a Promise chain, the return value of a `.then()` callback is passed as the argument to the next `.then()` callback in the chain.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, a variable that belongs to a class instance is called a:

options.label: A
options.text: Method

options.label: B
options.text: Function

options.label: C
options.text: Closure

options.label: D
options.text: Property or field

questions.explanationMd: In object-oriented programming, variables attached to class instances are referred to as properties or fields.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the `.preventDefault()` method on an event object?

options.label: A
options.text: To stop the browser from performing the default action associated with the event (like submitting a form).

options.label: B
options.text: To pause JavaScript execution until the user clicks

options.label: C
options.text: To prevent other elements from overlapping

options.label: D
options.text: To block cross-site scripting attacks

questions.explanationMd: Calling `event.preventDefault()` tells the browser not to execute its default behavior for that event, such as navigating to a new page on form submission or link click.

questions.questionMd: The `fetch()` API returns a Promise that resolves to what kind of object?

options.label: A
options.text: A JSON parsed string

options.label: B
options.text: An XML Document

options.label: C
options.text: A standard Array

options.label: D
options.text: A `Response` object representing the response to the request.

questions.explanationMd: The Promise returned from `fetch()` won't reject on HTTP error status even if the response is an HTTP 404 or 500. It resolves normally with a `Response` object.
